description: File is excessively large (4956 lines) with too many responsibilities, making it difficult to maintain and understand. Should be split into smaller, focused modules.
suggested_fix: Split routing.py into smaller files: one for route registration, one for request handling, one for response serialization, one for dependency resolution, etc.

description: File is excessively large (4749 lines) and acts as a god class, handling too many responsibilities. This violates the Single Responsibility Principle and makes the code harder to test and maintain.
suggested_fix: Refactor FastAPI class into smaller classes with single responsibilities. Extract OpenAPI handling, middleware setup, and route registration into separate classes or modules.

description: File is extremely large (24178 lines) and complex. This makes it difficult to audit for security vulnerabilities and maintain code quality.
suggested_fix: Split oauth2.py into smaller modules: one for OAuth2 password request forms, one for OAuth2 flow implementations, one for token handling, etc.

description: File is very large (13410 lines) and handles multiple HTTP security schemes. Could benefit from separation of concerns.
suggested_fix: Split http.py into separate modules for each authentication scheme (e.g., BasicAuth, DigestAuth, etc.).

description: High coupling: imports from 18 different internal modules and external libraries, making the class tightly coupled and harder to test in isolation.
suggested_fix: Reduce coupling by extracting dependencies and using dependency injection where possible. Group related imports into separate modules.

description: The `ai_vibes` method appears to be defined but may not be used anywhere in the codebase. Unused code increases maintenance burden and can cause confusion.
suggested_fix: Remove the `ai_vibes` method if it's not part of the public API or planned for future use. If it's a new feature, ensure it's properly integrated and used.
